tagline: Built-in support for Excel files enhances data ingestion capabilities.
what_it_does: Databricks now supports reading `.xls`, `.xlsx`, and `.xlsm` files natively, allowing users to ingest, parse, and query Excel files without needing external libraries or manual conversions. Users can read any sheet from multi-sheet workbooks, target specific cell ranges, and automatically infer schema and data types. This feature supports both batch and streaming workloads using Auto Loader, facilitating seamless data integration from Excel files.
why_it_matters: This enhancement significantly improves productivity by streamlining the data ingestion process for users who rely on Excel files, reducing the time and effort spent on data preparation. It allows data teams to leverage existing Excel data directly in their analytics workflows, enhancing decision-making capabilities and enabling faster insights. The built-in support also mitigates risks associated with manual conversions and external dependencies.
plain_summary: Databricks now supports reading .xls, .xlsx, and .xlsm files natively, allowing users to ingest, parse, and query Excel files without needing external libraries or manual conversions. Users can read any sheet from multi sheet workbooks, target specific cell…

capabilities.title: Native Excel Support
capabilities.desc: Read and process Excel files directly without external libraries.

capabilities.title: Batch and Streaming Workloads
capabilities.desc: Supports both batch and streaming data ingestion using Auto Loader.

capabilities.title: Schema Inference
capabilities.desc: Automatically infers schema and data types from Excel files.

capabilities.title: Targeted Data Access
capabilities.desc: Allows targeting specific sheets and cell ranges for precise data extraction.

use_cases: Ingesting financial reports stored in Excel for analysis.
Automating data workflows that require regular updates from Excel files.
Extracting data from complex Excel sheets with multiple tables.
Integrating Excel data into larger data pipelines for analytics.
limitations: Only one header row is supported.
Merged cell values only populate the top-left cell.
Schema evolution is not supported in streaming.
Strict Open XML Spreadsheet (Strict OOXML) is not supported.

steps.title: Check Runtime Version
steps.desc: Ensure your Databricks Runtime is 17.1 or above.

steps.title: Enable Feature
steps.desc: Workspace admins can enable Excel support from the Previews page.

steps.title: Upload Excel Files
steps.desc: Use the Add Data UI to upload Excel files or read from cloud storage.

steps.title: Read Excel Files
steps.desc: Use `spark.read.excel` to read Excel files in your notebooks.

key_takeaway: Excel file format support enhances data ingestion capabilities, enabling seamless integration of Excel data into analytics workflows.
